content:
# Static Corrections

**Summary**: The specific geophysics process of correcting for near-surface effects.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2011-toma|Toma, 2011]] developed a hybrid static-correction method to resolve cycle skips.

- [[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021]] aimed to improve static_corrections through accurate first arrival picking.

- [[2023-muhammad|Muhammad, 2023]] evaluated the impact of static-corrections on velocity estimation accuracy.

## Key Subtopics

- [[2011-toma|Toma, 2011]] ['high frequency statics', 'low frequency statics']

- [[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021]] aimed to improve static_corrections through accurate first arrival picking.

- [[2023-muhammad|Muhammad, 2023]] evaluated the impact of static-corrections on velocity estimation accuracy.

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[2011-toma|Toma, 2011]] ['seismic-processing']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The hybrid approach successfully resolved a major cycle skip in the Haba Field data. ([[2011-toma|Toma, 2011, p. 10]])

- Accurate first arrival traveltime is essential to enhance the efficiency of the static correction methods. ([[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021, p. 1]])

- Errors in statics correction can lead to up to a 35% error in estimated velocity. ([[2023-muhammad|Muhammad, 2023, p. 18]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[2011-toma|Toma, 2011]] Resolving cycle skips caused by multi-layer models.
